expectations.text: Flags 85% concentration as exceeding the 30% single-position threshold
expectations.evidence: Multiple references to >30% threshold. Summary: 'reducing KOG+KM concentration from 85% to 55-60% cuts your maximum single-event drawdown risk.' Screening table notes the demerger concentration risk.

expectations.text: Explains ASK tax rules: tax-free switching inside ASK, only taxed on withdrawal above total deposits
expectations.evidence: Dedicated appendix 'Why ASK Is Your Best Friend Here' with comparison table. Key line: 'Selling KOG inside ASK and buying index funds inside the same ASK = zero tax event.' Also quantifies: 'If you sold outside ASK, you would owe 37.84% * 1.13M = ~427,000 NOK in tax. Inside ASK, this tax is zero.'

expectations.text: Notes the additional risk of having both employment income AND portfolio concentrated in the same company
expectations.evidence: Adversarial research labels it 'the killer argument' and lists 5 correlated risks: stock drops, salary at risk, OTP pension hit, employee shares lose value, career market value drops — 'All five of these hit at the same time.'

expectations.text: Presents bull case (KOG/KM defense/maritime growth) and bear case (sector downturn, layoffs + portfolio loss simultaneously)
expectations.evidence: Full adversarial research table with 6 factors (defence spending, insider knowledge, demerger catalyst, tax efficiency, opportunity cost, career correlation) plus probability estimates (30% bull, 20% bear).

expectations.text: Considers gradual diversification vs immediate rebalancing
expectations.evidence: Phased approach: pre-demerger sales (1,000-1,200 shares), post-demerger KM partial sale, gradual rebalancing to target allocation by June 2026. Three scenarios compared: status quo, 55-60%, and 30%.

expectations.text: Models FI timeline impact of different portfolio allocations (concentrated vs diversified)
expectations.evidence: Three scenario tables (A: 85%, B: 55-60%, C: 30%) each projecting portfolio value and FI progress at 1, 5, 10, and 25 year horizons in both base and bear cases. Key insight: 'diversification saves you 5-10 years on the FI timeline' in bear case.

expectations.text: Conservative bias visible — recommends reducing concentration even if bull case is strong
expectations.evidence: Recommends Option B (55-60%) despite acknowledging 'defence spending is structural, 10+ year cycle.' Explicitly states: 'The asymmetry is the problem... In the bull case, you gain maybe 20-30% more. In the bear case, you could lose 40-60% of net worth.'

claims.text: Read KOG Demerger Strategy vault note and incorporated specific demerger timeline (April 22), share counts (3,600), and existing sell plan (1,000-1,200 shares)
Flagged insider trading quiet period as critical blocker — Q1 report April 30 may prevent pre-demerger trading
Quantified the tax savings of ASK rebalancing: ~427,000 NOK avoided vs. selling outside ASK
Connected to KM employee share program (20% discount in May) as part of rebalancing timeline

user_notes_summary: Web search unavailable; FI number estimated; exact cost basis unknown; insider trading status unresolved; Stage 5 skipped as eval run.
